question.text: When did SEBI first allow eligible foreign entities to hedge their positions in commodity derivatives in India?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** In October 2018, SEBI allowed eligible foreign entities to hedge their commodity derivatives positions if they had open positions in the underlying commodity in India from their business obligations. **The Trap:** 2015 was SEBI takeover of commodity regulation. Jan 2023 was the circular on multiple contract versions. May 2023 allowed FPI Direct Market Access. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series VIII covers FPI participation in equity derivatives. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** SEBI further expanded FPI access in 2022 — allowing participation without underlying exposure in cash-settled non-agricultural commodity derivatives through the FPI route.
